equivalence_argument: No complete implementation mapping was found in the audited local repo set: Missing the full bounded static commitment envelope for commit block, expiry, and historical lookback, and no stored commitment block/target-block comparison is available for the verifier to enforce.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing the full bounded static commitment envelope for commit block, expiry, and historical lookback, and no stored commitment block/target-block comparison is available for the verifier to enforce.
related_but_insufficient_code: No local crate exists at crates/ritual-async-commitment-validator/src/lib.rs in the port checkout.

crates/ritual-block-verification/src/lib.rs validate_commitment_structure checks TTL bounds, supported metadata, payload decoding, executor, and TTL equality, but it has no commit_block/current-block/historical-lookback envelope.
ritual-alloy-internal/crates/consensus/src/transaction/async_commitment.rs explicitly notes the simulation block is inferred at verification time and the transaction stores no commit_block.
crates/ethereum/payload/src/lib.rs replays parent-state async calls and checks count/address/input equivalence, but not a stored target or commitment block lookback bound.

rationale: The structural validator and block verifier candidates define both the static acceptance envelope and the stateful replay check that ties commitments to actual async precompile calls.
statement: Async commitments must pass bounded static checks for TTL, commit block, expiry, historical lookback, and supported async precompile address, then re-simulate the origin transaction at the commitment block and reject unless the detected target block, precompile address, and input match the commitment metadata.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct `TxAsyncCommitment` values with TTL 0, TTL 31, an unsupported precompile address, and valid HTTP/LLM commitments with TTL 1 and 30; assert only the supported address plus in-range TTL cases pass `validate_commitment_structure`.

Provide a state/env where the origin transaction calls an async precompile, then validate commitments with matching metadata, a different precompile address, different precompile input, and an origin transaction that makes no async call; assert only the matching re-simulation case returns `Ok(())`.
Validate commitments targeting each of the four named precompile constants and one arbitrary address; assert the named constants pass and the arbitrary address returns InvalidPrecompileAddress.

Construct blocks containing `TxAsyncCommitment` transactions with TTL 0, TTL above `ASYNC_MAX_BLOCK_LOOKBACK`, unsupported precompile address, `commit_block >= current_block`, `current_block - commit_block > ttl`, and `current_block - commit_block > 100`; assert each configured verifier rejects with `InvalidAsyncCommitment`, while a TTL within bounds and HTTP/LLM precompile proceeds to consistency validation.

Create an origin transaction that calls the HTTP or LLM precompile with a target block and input, build a matching commitment, and assert validation succeeds against historical state. Then mutate `commit_block`, `precompile_address`, or `precompile_input`, or use an origin transaction with no async call, and assert validation rejects with the corresponding mismatch/no-detection error.
summary: Commitment validation has TTL/address/input replay checks but lacks the full commit-block and lookback envelope, so this is a confirmed gap.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct a commitment whose origin transaction would only match outside the allowed historical window or with a future/stale commit block; current local commitment format and verifier lack the fields/checks needed to reject on that basis alone.
title: Async commitment validation binds supported precompile metadata to replay
